title: Constructors should avoid containing asynchronous operations
description: Constructors should not contain asynchronous operations. Constructors are meant to initialize an instance of a class synchronously. While you can technically run a promise in a constructor, the instance will be returned before the asynchronous operation completes, which can lead to potential issues if the rest of your code expects the object to be fully initialized.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant: class MyClass { constructor() { Promise.resolve().then(() => this.data = fetchData()); } }
compliant: class MyClass { constructor() { this.data = null; } async init() { this.data = await fetchData(); } }
function User() { this.isReady = false; } User.prototype.init = function() { setTimeout(() => this.isReady = true, 100); }

checks.message: Constructor should not contain asynchronous operations like Promise, await, setTimeout, etc.
Constructor function should not contain asynchronous operations like Promise, await, setTimeout, etc.
Constructor function variable should not contain asynchronous operations like Promise, await, setTimeout, etc.
